zh.New Brunswick, British Colonial America: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 布盧斯維其可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Benjamin Yerxa White I於1814年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 布盧斯維其可時，他的父親Thomas William White40歲，母親Elizabeth Sisson30歲。他於1835年12月5日與Margaret Jones在加拿大新布隆士威省約克道格拉斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1871年住在加拿大新布隆士威省約克昆斯波利。

zh-hans.New Brunswick, British Colonial America: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 布盧斯維其可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Benjamin Yerxa White I于1814年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 布盧斯維其可时，他的父亲Thomas William White40岁，母亲Elizabeth Sisson30岁。他于1835年12月5日与Margaret Jones在加拿大新布隆士威省約克道格拉斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他于1871年住在加拿大新布隆士威省約克昆斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Benjamin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the youngest of the twelve sons of Jacob. His mother Rachel died in giving birth to him, and in her last moments she named him Benoni, meaning ‘son of my sorrow’. His father, however, did not wish him to bear such an ill-omened name, and renamed him Benyamin (Genesis 35:16–18; 42:4). This means either ‘son of the right hand’ or more likely ‘son of the south’ (Hebrew yamin can also mean ‘south’), since Benjamin was the only child of Jacob born in Canaan and not in Mesopotamia to the north. Another tradition is that the second element of the name is a variant of the Hebrew plural noun yamim, which means ‘days’ but is used idiomatically to mean ‘year’ or ‘years’. The name would then mean ‘son of (my) old age’ and refer to the fact that Benjamin was Jacob's youngest child. In the Middle Ages the name was often given to sons whose mothers had died in childbirth. Today it has no such unfortunate associations and it grew enormously in popularity following the release of the film The Graduate ( 1967 ), in which Dustin Hoffman played the role of Benjamin Braddock . It is used in Scotland as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Beathan .

nameMeanings.name: Yerxa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Jurckse, a patronymic meaning ‘son of George’. The surname Jurckse is not found in the Netherlands.
History: Paulus Jurckse (1630–1682) arrived in New Netherland (now NY) in 1651 on Den Houttuyn, coming from Texel, the Netherlands.

historicEvents.title: First Bank
historicEvents.description: March 25, 1820, the first bank located in Saint John, New Brunswick, was established.

historicEvents.title: Aroostook War
historicEvents.description: The Aroostook War took place from 1838-1839. It was a conflict between the state of Maine and New Brunswick over the northern boarder. The battle was bloodless.

historicEvents.title: Marco Polo Launches
historicEvents.description: In 1851, New Brunswick became known for being home to the world's fastest clipper ship, the Marco Polo. It was a three masted clipper ship. It's maiden voyage was to Liverpool and only took 15 days. It sailed from Liverpool to Melbourne in 76 days.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Parks Canada Directory of Federal Heritage Designations- Career of the Marco Polo (Vessel)

historicEvents.title: Legislature in Fredericton Destoryed by Fire
historicEvents.description: On February 25, 1880, the legislature building in Frederiction was destroyed by fire. The builiding was completely made of wood meaning that there was nothing left of it. The chair that the speaker used and a marble top table were all that remained.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Canadian Parliamentary- One Hundred Years of History - The New Brunswick Legislative Building

historicEvents.title: Hartland Covered Bridge
historicEvents.description: July 4, 1901, the Hartland covered bridge was finished. It spans across the Saint John River, making it the longest covered bridge. Until it was built, the only way across the river was by ferry.

en.lifeSummary: When Benjamin Yerxa White I was born in 1814, in New Brunswick, British Colonial America, his father, Thomas William White, was 40 and his mother, Elizabeth Sisson, was 30. He married Margaret Jones on 5 December 1835, in Douglas, York, New Brunswick, Canada. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. He lived in Queensbury, York, New Brunswick, Canada in 1871.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Benjamin Yerxa White I est né en 1814, à Nouveau-Brunswick, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Thomas William White, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Sisson, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Jones le 5 décembre 1835, à Douglas, York, Nouveau-Brunswick, Canada. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Il a vécu à Queensbury, York, Nouveau-Brunswick, Canada en 1871.

de.lifeSummary: Als Benjamin Yerxa White I 1814 in New Brunswick, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas William White 40 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Sisson 30. Er hat am 5. Dezember 1835 in Douglas, York, New Brunswick, Kanada Margaret Jones geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1871 hat er in Queensbury, York, New Brunswick, Kanada gelebt.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Benjamin Yerxa White I è nato il nel 1814 nel Nuovo Brunswick, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Thomas William White, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Sisson, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Jones il 5 dicembre 1835, nel Douglas, York, Nuovo Brunswick, Canada. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a Queensbury, York, Nuovo Brunswick, Canada nel 1871.

ja.lifeSummary: Benjamin Yerxa White Iが1814年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ブルンスウィックで生まれた時，父Thomas William Whiteは40歳，母Elizabeth Sissonは30歳であった。1835年12月5日にカナダニューブランズウィック州ヨークドウグラスでMargaret Jonesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1871年にはカナダニューブランズウィック州ヨーククイーンスブリーに住んでいた。

ko.lifeSummary: Benjamin Yerxa White I(이)가 1814년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 브런스윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas William White(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Sisson(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1835년 12월 5일 캐나다 뉴브런즈윅 요르크 더글라스에서 Margaret Jones(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1871년도에 캐나다 뉴브런즈윅 요르크 퀸스버이에서 살았다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Benjamin Yerxa White I nasceu em 1814, em Novo Brunswick, British Colonial America, seu pai, Thomas William White, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Sisson, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Jones em 5 de dezembro de 1835, em Douglas, York, Novo Brunswick, Canadá. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele viveu em Queensbury, York, Novo Brunswick, Canadá em 1871.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Benjamin Yerxa White I родился в 1814 г. в местности Нью-Брансуик, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Thomas William White было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Sisson, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Jones 5 декабря 1835 г. в местности доуглас, ерк, Нью-Брансуик, Канада. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Он жил в местности куинсбури, ерк, Нью-Брансуик, Канада в 1871 году.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Benjamin Yerxa White I nació en 1814, en Nuevo Brunswick, British Colonial America, su papá, Thomas William White, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Sisson, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Margaret Jones el 5 de diciembre de 1835, en Douglas, York, Nuevo Brunswick, Canadá. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Vivió en Queensbury, York, Nuevo Brunswick, Canadá en 1871.
